ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-22-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF, GRANTED, PURSUANT TO CCP 36 (D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 4/5/2010 @ 11:15 AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATES AND GIVE NOTICE TO ALL DEFENDANTS. DISCOVERY CUT OFF IS 4/5/2010. ALL DISCOVERY MOTIONS TO BE NOTICED IN DEPT. 206, AND MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT TO BE NOTICED IN DEPT. 220. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 1/11/2010. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL RELY ON PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 2/1/2010. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-18-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Daniel B. Hoye, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4055), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Joyce Juelch, et al.

RULING:
Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Metalclad's alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence (issue 1), strict liability (issue 2), and loss of consortium (issue 5) and moot as to the remaining issues. Metalclad has not established all the elements of a workers compensation exclusivity defense, as Mrs. Juelch's alleged take-home exposure to asbestos that Mr. Juelch brought home on his work clothes did not arise out of and in the course of Mrs. Juelch's employment with Metalclad. Metalclad has not established all the elements of a sophisticated user defense since there is no evidence that Mrs. Juelch received any training in the hazards of asbestos prior to the commencement of her laundering of Mr. Juelch's work clothes. Mr. Juelch has a loss of consortium claim because Mrs. Juelch has a valid personal injury cause of action against Metalclad. To the extent that Metalclad's motion is based on an exposure/product identification issue, Metalclad did not satisfy its initial burden on that issue. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581
